h1: Saint Georges travel, stays & events guide
heroText: Saint Georges is a capital city in Grenada, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Saint Georges
highlights.text: Saint Georges is a capital city in Grenada, combining local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: George's was founded by the French in 1650 when "La Grenade" (Grenada) was colonised by Jacques Dyel du Parquet, the governor of Martinique.
In 1666, a wooden fortification was constructed by French colonists on a promontory overlooking Grenada's natural harbour and named Fort Royale.
In 1705, work started on a new star fort on the same site, with four stone-built bastions, to the design of Jean de Giou de Caylus, the Chief Engineer of the "Islands of America".
When the island was ceded to Great Britain by the Treaty of Paris in 1763, the new administration renamed it Saint George's Town, after the patron saint of England and Fort Royal was renamed.
After the great fire of 1771, most of the boarding houses on Granby Street were moved to Gouyave.
On 1 November 1775 there was a fire in the town of St.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: George's went on to become the replacement capital of the former colony of the British Windward Islands.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: George's and the rest of Grenada was severely battered by Hurricane Ivan.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: A year later with the help of international donors (Canada, the United States, China, Venezuela, Trinidad & Tobago, and the European Union) Grenada had experienced a significant turn around in rebuilding efforts.
